title: Sub-step 3a: Update travelogue fetch logic to use journeyId

requirements.summary: Update the travelogue data fetch logic so that travelogue entries are queried and loaded by journeyId rather than by city name or any global/shared key
acceptanceCriteria: The travelogue fetch function/hook accepts a journeyId parameter and uses it as the lookup key when calling the backend
Each journey's travelogue data is fetched independently — two journeys sharing the same city return their own separate travelogue entries
No other frontend components, panels, layouts, or styles are altered by this change
The travelogue save, delete, and clear paths remain untouched

file_operations.description: Modify the useGetTravelogueEntry hook to accept a journeyId parameter instead of a city parameter. Update the queryKey to use journeyId and change the queryFn to call actor.getTravelogueEntry(journeyId) instead of any city-based lookup. Update the enabled condition to check for journeyId presence. Preserve all other hooks and mutation functions unchanged.

Update the TraveloguePanel component to derive journeyId from the selected journey object and pass journeyId to useGetTravelogueEntry instead of city. Ensure the journeyId is extracted from the Journey type (likely journey.title or a computed ID based on title+dates). Do not modify any layout, styling, or unrelated UI elements.
